title: weakrefobject.c:  UNWRAP ref leak with dead proxy
author: prakashsellathurai
body:
# Bug report

### Bug description:

# Bug report

### Bug description:

Parent: https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146102

https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/f7d0c7694b2be2345f4cf85f4eedc3ee

## Summary

When first `UNWRAP(x)` succeeds but second `UNWRAP(y)` fails (dead proxy), `x`'s new reference is leaked. Affects ~20 binary operators (+, -, *, /, |, &, ^, etc.).


 



## Reproducer

```python
import weakref, sys, gc

class C:
    def __add__(self, other): return NotImplemented
    def __radd__(self, other): return NotImplemented

obj = type('D', (), {})()
dead = weakref.proxy(obj)
del obj; gc.collect()

live_obj = C()
live = weakref.proxy(live_obj)

before = sys.gettotalrefcount()
for i in range(10000):
    try:
        live + dead
    except ReferenceError:
        pass
after = sys.gettotalrefcount()
print(f"Leaked {after - before} refs (~{(after-before)//10000}/call)")
```
which gives 

```
(venv) PS C:\Users\Prakash\Documents\work\projects\cpython>
>> .\python.bat  .\tmp\test.py
Running Debug|x64 interpreter...
Leaked 10003 refs (~1/call)
```




### CPython versions tested on:

CPython main branch

### Operating systems tested on:

_No response_

<!-- gh-linked-prs -->
### Linked PRs
* gh-148308
* gh-148486
<!-- /gh-linked-prs -->

comments.author: prakashsellathurai
comments.body:
upon investigation found the issue in ternary operator also


test script https://gist.github.com/prakashsellathurai/69d9d3ed90c5975a343e36f08e437046

output  (main line)


```

(venv) PS C:\Users\Prakash\Documents\work\projects\mainline>  .\python.bat  ..\cpython\tmp\ternar-test.py
Running Debug|x64 interpreter...
pow(live, dead)      leaked 10003 refs (~1/call)
pow(live, live, dead) leaked 20000 refs (~2/call)
pow(dead, live, live) leaked 0 refs
```

comments.author: prakashsellathurai
comments.body:
problem is in UNWRAP https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/d8c56581560ed139d261dd14acd73597e40cf88b/Objects/weakrefobject.c#L541-L542

where Unwrap macro returns early if the object is null

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: Not entirely sure that the fix is trivial. There is a kind of steal-borrow thing behind the scene here. UNWRAP is called on strong references so we should be careful. Instead, we should perhaps add a "cleanup" C suite to perform on error instead of directly returning NULL.

comments.author: prakashsellathurai
comments.body:
Above comment from me is just a reference note for me  not the fix . 

Thanks for pointing out about  reference cleanups.
I've actually already handled those in the latest push of my draft pr.
